assertion_results.assertion: Data extraction identifies the ecosystem as RPM based on the library name (openssl-libs) and component context, not Cargo (Step 1 Ecosystem detection — read ecosystems from Ecosystem Mappings config)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md lines 30-43: Identifies openssl-libs as 'a system-level RPM package (not a Cargo crate or npm package)', consults the Ecosystem Mappings table from security-matrix.md, and concludes 'The detected ecosystem is **RPM**.' The ecosystem is read from the config table, not hardcoded.

assertion_results.assertion: Version impact table includes ALL versions from the 2.2.x stream supportability matrix (2.2.0 through 2.2.4) and uses rpms.lock.yaml data for version extraction (Step 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md: Supportability matrix (lines 7-13) lists all 5 versions: 2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4. Dependency version extraction (lines 19-25) uses rpms.lock.yaml as the data source for each pinned tag. Version impact table (lines 35-41) includes all 5 versions with affected/not-affected status.

assertion_results.assertion: Dependency chain context identifies openssl-libs as present in rpms.lock.yaml and uses this to determine the package origin — the lock file presence is the primary classification signal (Step 2.3.5)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md lines 47-51: 'openssl-libs is present in rpms.lock.yaml for the affected versions (2.2.0 through 2.2.2). The rpms.lock.yaml presence is the primary classification signal.' Classifies as 'explicit install' based on lock file presence. Line 68: 'The rpms.lock.yaml classification is the primary signal.'

assertion_results.assertion: Remediation creates a single task for the Konflux release repo — not the two-task upstream backport + downstream propagation flow used for source dependency ecosystems (Step 8 Remediation Task Creation — system package path)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md lines 7-9: 'the remediation follows the **single-task path**. Only one remediation task is created in the Konflux release repo. There is no upstream backport + downstream propagation split.' Only one task description is provided. Lines 146-147: 'Single task, not two: RPM is a system package ecosystem.'

assertion_results.assertion: Remediation task description follows task-description-template.md format with Repository, Target Branch, Description, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections — Files to Modify is intentionally omitted per remediation-templates.md (Step 8 Remediation Task Creation)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md lines 17-55: Task description contains Repository (line 19: 'rhtpa-release.0.4.z'), Target Branch (line 23: 'main'), Description (line 27), Implementation Notes (line 36), Acceptance Criteria (line 43). Line 57: 'The Files to Modify section is intentionally omitted per remediation-templates.md.'

assertion_results.assertion: The version impact output addresses SBOM verification in the dependency chain context — either noting that verification was skipped because cosign is not available or because external tools are prohibited, or presenting the SBOM classification result (Step 2.3.5 Optional SBOM verification)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md lines 54-55: 'SBOM verification skipped -- cosign not available. External tool invocations are prohibited in this eval context. Using rpms.lock.yaml classification only.' Lines 67-69 reinforce: 'The SBOM verification step was skipped because cosign is not available in this environment. The rpms.lock.yaml data alone is sufficient to determine the remediation path.'
